sanganbs: Sanganeb Atoll is a UNESCO World Heritage Site located 25 km northeast of Port Sudan, approximately 2 hours by boat. This pristine coral reef system is crowned by a historic British lighthouse built in 1906, still operational today. The atoll is renowned for its dramatic vertical walls dropping over 800 meters into the deep blue, offering some of the Red Sea's most spectacular diving experiences. Crystal-clear waters with visibility exceeding 30 meters reveal an underwater paradise teeming with marine life. Divers frequently encounter hammerhead sharks, grey reef sharks, manta rays, dolphins, and sea turtles gliding through vibrant coral gardens. Strong currents bring nutrient-rich waters, attracting large pelagic species and creating thrilling drift dives. Sanganeb remains one of Sudan's most iconic and unspoiled diving destinations, perfect for experienced divers seeking adventure.
umbriastory1: Umbria was a massive ship, completed and launched for the first time in 1911 in Hamburg, Germany, as a cargo and passenger vessel. It was over 150 meters long, accommodating 2,000 passengers and over 9,000 tons of cargo. It operated between Argentina and Europe under the name Bahia Blanca. In 1935, the Italian government purchased it and renamed it Umbria. Italy used it to transport goods and soldiers to its colonies in East Africa via the Suez Canal, passing through Sudanese waters. When World War II broke out in May 1940, Italy remained neutral, but the ship secretly carried cargo to support the defense of Italian colonies. The cargo was massive and valuable at the time: 360,000 pieces of explosives (ranging from 15 kg to 100 kg), 60 boxes of dynamite and fuses, construction materials, a total of 8,600 tons of weapons, in addition to three luxury Fiat Lancia cars. With this secret cargo, Umbria sailed towards Massawa (as Eritrea was an Italian colony at the time). From there, the cargo was supposed to be distributed to different parts of Asia. To cover its mission, 1,000 tons of coal and water were loaded in Port Said to make it look like a regular cargo ship. Britain, which controlled the Suez Canal, delayed the ship for three days to inspect the cargo. It knew that Italy was about to join the war, but since it was still neutral, it was allowed to carry weapons to its colonies. After the inspection, Britain allowed the ship to pass under British naval escort.
umbriastory2: When interrogated, the British asked the captain why he scuttled the ship. He simply replied: 'Because you are our enemies!' A day later, the entire crew was transferred to India where they spent four years in prison. Today, Umbria rests intact on the seabed, 30 km off the coast of Port Sudan. What distinguishes it is that it did not suffer any destruction; it still retains all its cargo and the explosives within it. Shipwreck enthusiasts consider it one of the most beautiful sunken ships in the world, as its entire hull is still preserved, which is rare. Coral reefs have grown over it, adding extra beauty. Divers can still see Umbria's cargo and the reef formations inside it. However, Italian sources warn divers against touching anything, because the massive amount of explosives on board could destroy Port Sudan if detonated. Whether this is fact or exaggeration remains unclear. Most visitors to Umbria are Italian tourists, who see it as a heroic national symbol. The abundance of marine life, coral reefs, and clear waters of the area also attract divers, making it a truly rare and special diving destination.
umbriastory3: Three days later, Umbria entered Sudanese waters. There, once again, the British Navy ordered it to stop off the coast of Port Sudan under the pretext of smuggling. A crew of 20 British officers boarded the ship and inspected it thoroughly without finding anything suspicious. They then asked the Italians to stay on board overnight. The Italian captain realized that Britain would seize the ship and use its cargo against his country, and he knew he had only a few hours before Britain officially declared war on Italy, as news spread slowly back then. He acted quickly and ordered his crew to start emergency procedures. The British saw this as reasonable, thinking it would only delay the ship, and helped with the evacuation, boarding lifeboats with the Italian crew. Meanwhile, Captain Muiesan ordered his engineers to open all valves and deliberately scuttle the ship in the same spot. Slowly and intentionally, Umbria sank to the seabed loaded with its precious cargo—sacrificed for Italy.
umbriahead: The Full Story of the Sunken Ship Umbria off the Coast of Port Sudan
romy: Shabab Al‑Rumi” is the name of one of the world’s most famous coral reefs, located about 48 km north of Port Sudan in Sudan. The area is renowned as a global diving destination because of its stunning beauty and diverse coral formations, as well as the historic dive by the famous diver Jack Kusto in 1963. Location: North‑east of Port Sudan Type: One of the most prominent and distinctive circular coral reefs in the world Significance: Widely celebrated as a premier diving spot for its beauty and coral diversity History: Contains the remnants of the “Continental Shelf 2” expedition carried out by Jack Kusto in 1963, during which the film “World Without Sun” was shot Protection: Situated within the buffer zone of a UNESCO World Natural Heritage site in the Red Sea, between the Sunqaneeb and Dongnab nature reserves.
irg: Arq Al‑Nuhas is a coral reef area located on the coast of Port Sudan in the Red Sea. It is renowned for its rich, colorful coral formations that cover the seabed with thousands of species. The water is clear and the temperature stays moderate year‑round, making it ideal for scuba diving. Divers can see rainbow‑tang fish, sea turtles, and a wide variety of vividly colored corals. The site offers multiple dive spots suitable for all skill levels, from beginners to advanced divers. There is little tourist infrastructure, so the area retains its natural, tranquil character. Because of its natural beauty and biodiversity, Arq Al‑Nuhas is a favorite destination for diving enthusiasts and marine explorers.
team: Blue Shark Diving & Marine Services is a Port Sudan–based company specializing in inshore and offshore marine operations. We provide a full range of professional services including underwater inspections, hull cleaning, vessel support,and technical diving solutions.Alongside our core operations, we organize diving trips, campingexperiences, and internationally recognized diving courses, combining adventurewith expertise in the heart of the Red Sea.With a skilled team, international standards, and a strong commitment to safety and reliability, Blue Shark is your trusted partner—above and below the water
goos2: In 2014, Blue Shark Marine Services supervised the training of the first Open Water diving course. The course was organized under the direct supervision of the company's General Manager to ensure the quality of instruction. The program focused on delivering practical experience as well as basic and advanced diving skills. Participants received accredited international certifications under the CMAC system The training was distinguished by high safety standards and strict adherence to international diving regulations. This course contributed significantly to raising the level of marine competencies among the participants and was considered a vital step in enhancing the company's position in the field of professional diver training.
shape2: In 2013, Blue Shark Marine Services executed its first complete refloating operation of a sunken object. The operation took place at a depth of up to 12 meters below the surface. The company leveraged the expertise of its General Manager, Captain Geddo Madani, who supervised every stage of the operation with high precision and skill. The project was distinguished by professional planning and organized execution to ensure safety Such an achievement reflects the company’s capability to handle challenging marine operations. It marks the beginning of a series of successes in the field of diving and the refloating of sunken objects.
ut2: Blue Shark Marine Services executed a precise technical mission for the MV Dahab, a vessel belonging to the Sudanese Maritime Lines, with the objective of assessing the hull condition and inspecting corrosion levels. The team utilized UT – Ultrasonic Thickness Measurement technology to accurately measure the steel thickness in various parts of the hull below the waterline. The diving and inspection operations were carried out in compliance with internationally adopted safety and quality standards to ensure reliable results The scope of work included comprehensive coverage of all required inspection points, in addition to analyzing areas that showed signs of corrosion or structural weakness Following the completion of the measurements and underwater documentation, the company prepared a detailed technical report outlining the current hull status and providing necessary recommendations for maintenance and repair This project reflected the expertise of the Blue Shark team in advanced underwater technical work, particularly in the field of Non-Destructive Surveys. This achievement represents a new addition to the company's track record in supporting the safety and continuity of the Sudanese maritime fleet.
ship: Locating the propeller of the vessel Noor Jeddah
ship2: Following the collision incident involving the MV Noor Jeddah and the subsequent loss of one of its main propellers, numerous attempts to locate the missing blade proved unsuccessful.  the challenging location and complex underwater search conditions, the Blue Shark Marine Services team continued working non-stop. In an exceptional achievement, the team successfully located the blade in just one day, leveraging their expertise and advanced technique rch operations were carried out by a team of professional divers who executed a precise seabed survey around the incident area. This success reflected the high level of professionalism and discipline the company maintains when dealing with marine emergencie rk received great appreciation for the significant effort the team exerted under complex operational circumstance God for mercy and forgiveness for our colleague and comrade Mohaned Christian, who left an unforgettable mark in this field
sunck2: Blue Shark Marine Services executed its first complete clearance operation of a sunken object located approximately 2 kilometers offshore, opposite the jetty. The team initiated the work with survey and assessment procedures to determine the condition of the sunken object and devise a safe plan for handling it. This was followed by a precise cutting operation, dividing the object into six sections in compliance with approved marine safety standardsUpon completion of the cutting, the company utilized the Ports' floating crane to hoist the heavy sections from the seabed. Each part was carefully salvaged to ensure no damage to the marine environment or navigational channelsThis project underscored Blue Shark’s capability to execute complex operations demanding high technical expertise and specialized equipment, marking this achievement as a significant milestone in the company's track record in marine clearance and underwater salvage.
africa: In 2020, Blue Shark Marine Services executed a sophisticated diving and marine survey operation in collaboration with the Egyptian Survey Company. Blue Shark’s team of divers and technicians spearheaded professional diving operations and underwater photography for the new Africa1 Port Sudan subsea cable project site in Port Sudan. The scope of work included seabed sampling, marine soil analysis, and technical penetration testing to assess site readiness for the cable installation. All phases were conducted using precision techniques and specialized equipment to ensure reliable results compliant with international standards. This project played a vital role in supporting Sudan’s telecommunications infrastructure and enhancing its regional and global connectivity. Through this operation, Blue Shark demonstrated its high efficiency in managing sensitive maritime projects, adding another significant milestone to its growing track record of delivering safe and expert marine solutions.
asina2: After a continuous operation lasting more than 17 days, in extremely challenging marine conditions with strong currents, high water pressure, and almost zero visibility, a Sudanese team from Blue Shark Diving and Red Sea Secrets companies achieved an exceptional feat. They succeeded in rescuing the #ASINA_CARGO ship, which had completely sunk to a depth of over 16 meters in the Red Sea, in an operation described as one of the most precise and professional marine missions 🏊🥽🦺. The operation proved beyond doubt that Sudanese professionals 💪 are capable of carrying out the most complex marine rescue operations with efficiency that matches international standards. This success was not just a ship rescue, but a message of pride in Sudan's maritime capabilities. The national teams proved that professionalism is not exclusive to outsiders, and that the people of this country are capable of bringing life back to sunken iron in the depths of the Red Sea through willpower, knowledge, and faith in their abilities.
gido_desc: In 2018, Blue Shark Marine Services achieved one of its most significant accomplishments in the field of heavy marine operations. The company undertook the cutting and salvage of the Al-Rashid ship, owned by Baaboud Shipping Company, after it sank near Bashayer Port and posed many risks to navigation in the area. The Blue Shark team carried out the mission using specialized equipment and high-precision techniques to ensure the safe removal of the wreck without impacting the marine environment. The operation spanned several stages, including initial surveying, engineering planning, cutting, and then the complete salvage of the wreck. This achievement marked a milestone in the company's record, confirming its ability to execute complex projects with high efficiency. Thanks to this success, Blue Shark has established itself as one of the leading companies specializing in marine services in Sudan and the Red Sea.

Our experienced offshore team operates with strict safety standards and uses advanced  technology to deliver efficient, reliable solutions at sea.

Garbage and Waste Disposal – Environmentally compliant waste collection Bunker Coordination – Assisting with fuel supply arrangements Crew Change Assistance – Helping with immigration transport, nd accommodation Our experienced team ensures your vessel’s operational readiness, safety, and  compliance with international maritime regulations

mision: 🎯 Our Mission To provide innovative diving and marine tourism services, with a focus on safety, quality, and marine environmental preservation, in order to meet the needs of both individuals and companies, and to enhance marine tourism in the Sudanese Red Sea.
vision: 🌊 Our Vision To be the first choice in Sudan for diving and marine services, by offering unique and safe experiences, professional diving education, and comprehensive ship maintenance and supply services, with a strong commitment to environmental sustainability
info: 🐟 About Us Blue Shark Diving and Marine Services is a leading Sudanese company specialized in providing both commercial and recreational diving services in addition to marine maintenance ship supply, and expedition trips in the Red Sea. The company was founded with the aim of promoting the culture of diving and delivering high-quality marine services while adhering to safety standards and protecting the marine environment.

masharf-story: Masharif Island 🏝️ a well-known tourist destination in Port Sudan, famous for its beautiful sandy beaches and clear waters. It is an ideal place for diving enthusiasts and marine activities such as boating. It is not just a single island but part of M.Gool . Information about the Island Activities: Ideal for diving and swimming Environment: Characterized by beautiful sandy beaches and clear waters Marine Activities: Includes boating and other marine activities
durah-story: 🏝️ About Darh Island: Location : Located south of Port Sudan  relatively close to other islands such as Fajja and Selada. It is usually accessed by small boats. sNature: A sandy–rocky island surrounded by very clear waters and coral reefs making it an attractive spot for divers and sea trips. Marine Life: The coral reefs around the island are rich in colorful fish small reef sharks and occasionally dolphins can be seen in the area. Activities: Diving and snorkeling Traditional fishing Simple camping and day trip Tourism Status: The island is not developed for tourism meaning there are no ready-made services. It is a destination for natural trips and for those who love adventure and tranquility. ✨ In short: Darh Island is a pristine natural treasure among the Red Sea islands, perfect for diving, fishing, and quiet sea trips away from the crowds.
salada-story: Selada Island 🏝️ About the Island: Location: Situated northeast of Port Sudan it is one of the relatively close islands to the shore making it easily accessible by fishing boats or tourist vessels. Nature: The island is surrounded by pristine coral reefs and clear turquoise waters, making it an attractive spot for diving and snorkeling. Marine Life: Known for its variety of fish and coral reefs, in addition to some seabirds that use it as a resting place. Local Use: Some fishermen use it as a resting stop during fishing trips, and occasionally as a simple camping area. Tourism Potential: The island has no developed infrastructure or tourist services, which makes it a suitable destination for natural trips for those seeking tranquility and privacy. ✨ In short: Selada Island is a promising destination for simple sea trips and diving around coral reefs, and it is part of the hidden treasures of Sudan’s Red Sea.
faja-story: Faja Island 🏝️ Location: Located near Port Sudan, it is one of the small islands scattered along the coast. Access is usually by small boats from the port or nearby marinas. Nature: The island is surrounded by crystal-clear waters and diverse coral reefs, making it a popular destination for diving and snorkeling enthusiasts. Marine Life: Home to a wide variety of colorful fish and coral reefs, and occasionally dolphins and sea turtles can be spotted in its waters. Activities: Ideal for short trips, simple camping, diving, and swimming. There is little infrastructure, so the place remains close to its natural wilderness. Climate: Shares the same coastal climate (hot and dry most of the year), but the sea breeze makes the weather more pleasant, especially in the evenings. 🔹 In short, Faja Island is a perfect spot for marine nature lovers, especially those who enjoy camping and diving.
course1: Start your underwater adventure today! Get your internationally recognized diving certification with our expert instructors. We offer comprehensive training that combines theory and practice to ensure you become a confident and safe diver.
kidcourse1: Spark your child's curiosity with our fun and safe introduction to diving! Designed specifically for kids, this course teaches basic skills in a controlled environment, building confidence and a love for the ocean.
price_describe_sanganeb: Imagine snorkeling in the crystal-clear waters of Sanganeb Island, where every dive feels like entering another world.and your every moment is captured through stunning underwater photography, preserving the magic of your Red Sea adventure forever.
price_describe_irg: Dive into the mesmerizing waters of Irg Alnahas Coral Reef, where the sea bursts with color and life. As you swim through the crystal-blue waves, you’ll be surrounded by brilliant coral formations and vibrant tropical fish gliding gracefully beside you.
price_describe_cambing: Experience unforgettable camping adventures in Port Sudan, where the golden desert meets the turquoise sea.Set up your tent under a blanket of stars on remote Red Sea islands or along the peaceful Sudanese coast.From sandy dunes to coral-fringed shores, every spot offers a unique blend of tranquility, adventure, and natural beauty.
price_describe_umbria: Dive deep into history at the Umbria Wreck, an Italian ship resting about 30 meters beneath the Red Sea near Port Sudan. This legendary site offers an unforgettable deep-diving experience, where coral-covered hulls hide relics of World War II.As you explore the sunken chambers and cargo, you’ll feel the thrill of touching a frozen moment in time beneath crystal-clear waters
price_describe_romy: Dive into the mesmerizing waters of Al-Romy Coral Reef, where the sea bursts with color and life. As you swim through the crystal-blue waves, you’ll be surrounded by brilliant coral formations and vibrant tropical fish gliding gracefully beside you.
price_describe_spicial: Design your own exclusive Red Sea adventure. Whether you seek a private getaway, a specific diving location, or a unique celebration, our Special Trip allows you to customize every detail for a truly personal and unforgettable experience.
arkweet-story: Ar Kaweit 🏝️ , also spelled as Arkawit or Erkowit, is a small town in eastern Sudan. It is around 40 kilometres (25 mi) from the Red Sea and around 90–95 kilometres (56–59 mi) from Sudan's 2nd largest city, Port Sudan. It is at a 1,000–1,200 metres (3,300–3,900 ft) elevation sitting atop a flat mountain. Nearby towns are Carthago, Sinkat, and Gebeit. It is also approximately 470 kilometres (290 mi) from the Nile River. It has a mosque, a school, and a park near it.[3] On 25 November 1942, a Royal Air Force crashed near Ar Kaweit. The crew set out from Wadi Gazouza Airport for a training exercise. Regrettably, the aircraft did not make it back, and on 31 December, its wreckage was located near Ar Kaweit. This incident resulted in the loss of all three crew members: Sgt Clive Colin Dunstone, Sgt John Skinner, and Sgt James Murray Thomson.
aroos-story: The Arous 🏝️ area is a tourist village located on the Red Sea coast, north of Port Sudan in eastern Sudan. It is renowned as a popular resort for diving and viewing coral reefs. It is a wonderful area for stargazing under clear skies, and its waters are extremely pure. Location: Situated north of Port Sudan on the Red Sea coast Tourist Features: A prominent tourist destination for diving enthusiasts, featuring beautiful coral reefs Environment: Characterized by crystal-clear sea waters and offers charming landscapes, especially for stargazing at night History: Arous village was the subject of a film regarding an operation conducted by the Israeli Mossad to transport thousands of Falasha Jews across the Red Sea in 1981.
mgool-story: Muhammad Qol 🏝️ City Muhammad Qol is a coastal city in Sudan, located on the Red Sea coast and administratively part of the Red Sea State. The city is famous for its old port, which played a significant role in fishing and fish exports, and is considered part of the Greater Port Sudan area. Location: Situated on the Red Sea coast, south of Port Sudan Economic Activity: Historically renowned for its port, which was used for exporting fish Administrative Affiliation: It is considered part of the Port Sudan locality, which includes units such as Central, South, and East Port Sudan Muhammad Qol Fort (Tabia): A building that resembles a fortress more than a standard Tabia (defensive outpost). It has two floors, a wooden roof, and observation windows. Built with high-durability stone, it is distinct from the Khedivial and Mahdist forts and serves as a testament to defensive architecture in Eastern Sudan
